sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Is BJ's Restaurants Inc (BJRI) a Bargain After 3.2% Drop? GF Value Says Undervalued
feed.summary: BJ's Restaurants Inc (BJRI) shares recently dropped 3.2%, closing at $37.40, which GuruFocus' proprietary GF Value™ suggests is slightly undervalued, with a GF Value™ of $38.01. The company holds a strong GF Score™ of 82/100, driven by excellent valuation and good profitability, though its growth potential is moderate. While insider activity shows a balanced buying and selling, the stock's current P/E ratio of 17.4x is considerably lower than its 5-year median, indicating a potential historical undervaluation.

feed.title: Starbucks Is Selling a New Protein-Packed Breakfast Item
feed.summary: Starbucks has launched a new protein-packed breakfast option nationwide: MUSH Chocolate Peanut Butter Protein Overnight Oats. This ready-to-eat item, which was a 2025 Good Housekeeping Snack Award winner, offers 15 grams of protein and up to 7 grams of fiber. A dietitian praised the new offering for being a nutritious, convenient, and gluten-free breakfast solution for busy mornings.

feed.title: Future of E waste Management Market (2026-2033) | Recycling
feed.summary: The E-Waste Management Market is projected to grow from USD 89.6 billion in 2025 to USD 244.6 billion by 2033, driven by increasing electronic consumption and shorter product lifecycles. The market's growth is fueled by advancements in recycling technologies, stringent regulations, and circular economy initiatives, despite challenges like informal recycling and high processing costs. Key players like Waste Management Inc. and Veolia Environnement are expanding capabilities, with Asia Pacific holding the largest market share.

feed.title: SLB and Subsea7 sign collaboration with Petronas Suriname
feed.summary: SLB and Subsea7 have entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with Petronas Suriname to develop cost-effective subsea solutions for Suriname's emerging frontier basin. This long-term partnership aims to accelerate field development, improve project economics, and leverage early involvement for co-creation. The Subsea Integration Alliance may provide full EPCIC solutions, including subsea production systems and SURF components, for multiple discoveries in the region.

feed.title: Array Board Overhaul And End Of Staggered Terms Might Change The Case For Investing In Array (ARRY)
feed.summary: Array Technologies (ARRY) is undergoing a significant corporate governance shift with seven independent directors not seeking re-election and a plan to declassify its board by 2029. This overhaul towards annual director elections could enhance accountability and influence investor perception of the company. While these changes may indirectly impact how management addresses market challenges, the company's investment narrative remains heavily tied to solar tracker demand and its ability to achieve profitable growth amidst policy and project uncertainties.

feed.title: What Waste Management (WM)'s Earnings, Buybacks and Dividend Track Record Means For Shareholders
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) is garnering increased investor attention ahead of its April 28, 2026 earnings release, with analysts expecting higher quarterly and full-year profit and revenue. The company's ongoing share repurchase program and consistent dividend increases are also key highlights. Investors are evaluating WM's potential for profit growth, sustained capital returns, and investments in recycling and renewable natural gas, especially in light of increased leverage after the Stericycle acquisition.
